avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong southwesterly wind, avalanche prone wind slabs formed. They can be released easily and reach very large size. This applies in particular on steep slopes above the tree line, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. In many cases avalanches can be triggered in the various wind slab layers.
It is inadvisable to engage in backcountry touring outside marked and open pistes.
avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs represent the main danger. The conditions are very dangerous.

snowpackStructure.comment: Over a wide area over a wide area 30 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the early morning. Down to low altitudes snow will fall. As a consequence of the strong southwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed. Over a wide area new snow and wind slabs are lying on a weakly bonded old snowpack.

avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind, large wind slabs formed. Wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. This applies in particular on steep slopes, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain also at intermediate altitudes.
Avalanches can be released in deeper layers in particular on steep shady slopes. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches are a clear indication of a weakly bonded snowpack.
Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Considerable avalanche danger will prevail. The new snow and wind slabs represent the main danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area over a wide area 15 to 25 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until midday. As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind, further wind slabs will form. The new snow and wind slabs will be deposited on soft layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on very steep shady slopes.

Avalanches can be released in deeper layers in particular on steep shady slopes. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches are a clear indication of a weakly bonded snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Considerable avalanche danger will prevail. The new snow and wind slabs represent the main danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area over a wide area 20 to 30 cm of snow will fall. As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind, further wind slabs will form. The new snow and wind slabs will be deposited on soft layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on very steep shady slopes.
